pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's Java source and target compatibility from version 11 to 17.
Upgrade the Keycloak library version to 22.0.0 to align with the new Java version and feature set.
Upgrade the testcontainers-keycloak library to version 3.0.0 to support newer Keycloak versions.
Upgrade the Resteasy library to version 6.2.4.Final, which is required for Jakarta EE / Keycloak 22 compatibility.
Upgrade the Docker Maven Plugin to version 0.40.2.
Refactor the module structure to organize extensions, apps, and docker configurations into new directories.
Rename the root artifact ID to keycloak-project-example to better reflect its purpose.
Introduce parameterized versioning using revision and changelist properties.
Add Freemarker as a managed dependency for extensions.
Update Docker image configuration to use a new image name and file path.

.gitignore.general_idea: Add gitignore entries for local runtime directories to prevent committing generated state.
Ignore Infinispan data directories for local cluster deployments to avoid committing cache state.
Ignore generated TLS certificates and keys to prevent committing sensitive or transient security artifacts.
Ignore local environment configuration files to prevent committing local secrets or settings.
Ignore legacy Thorntail module configuration files that are no longer relevant.
Ignore Keycloak import/export data directories to prevent committing large or transient test data.
Ignore specific TLS certificate paths in the dev stage configuration.
Remove the generic bin/ ignore rule to allow tracking specific binaries in the bin directory.
Allow the OpenTelemetry Java agent JAR to be tracked in the repository for consistent tracing setup.
Add standard Node.js gitignore rules to handle frontend tooling artifacts.
Ignore data directories for offline session client examples to prevent committing test data.
Ignore session data directories in local cluster deployments to prevent committing transient state.

readme.md.general_idea: Rename the project title from 'Template' to 'Example' to better reflect its purpose as a comprehensive demonstration.
Rewrite the introduction to clarify the project's role in supporting the full lifecycle of Keycloak-based development, including extensions, themes, and configuration.
readme.md.human_impl: +This setup serves as a starting point to support the full lifecycle of development in a keycloak based project.

readme.md.general_idea: Add a new section detailing specific use cases for developers, testers, and operators to clarify the project's versatility.
readme.md.human_impl:
+## Use-Cases
+a) **Developer** for keycloak themes, extensions and image

readme.md.general_idea: Update the highlights section to list specific extensions (SMS, Backup-Codes, etc.) and remove generic descriptions.
readme.md.human_impl: +- Extensions: SMS Authenticator, Backup-Codes, Remote Claim Mapper, Audit Event Listener, and Custom REST Endpoint

readme.md.general_idea: Add numerous new feature highlights including E2E tests, Config-as-Code, LDAP, MailHog, TLS, Metrics, Clustering, and Tracing.
readme.md.human_impl:
+- Example for End-to-End Tests with [Cypress](https://www.cypress.io/)
+- Realm configuration as Configuration as Code via [keycloak-config-cli]

readme.md.general_idea: Add a table specifying the required environment versions, explicitly stating Java 17.
Refine build instructions and remove the specific docker-maven-plugin command in favor of general maven commands.
Introduce a platform-agnostic Java launcher script (`start.java`) for starting the Keycloak environment.
readme.md.human_impl: +We provide a platform-agnostic single-file source-code Java launcher [start.java](start.java) to start the Keycloak environment.

readme.md.general_idea: Add documentation for enabling HTTPS support using mkcert and configuring hostnames.
readme.md.human_impl:
+### Enable HTTPS
+Generate a certificate and Key for the example domain `acme.test` with [mkcert]

readme.md.general_idea: Add documentation for enabling OpenLDAP integration via a command-line flag.
Add documentation for switching the database from H2 to PostgreSQL via a command-line flag.
Add documentation for running the legacy Wildfly-based Keycloak distribution instead of the default Quarkus distribution.
Add instructions on how to access metrics via the Wildfly management interface.
readme.md.human_impl:
+### Access metrics
+Metrics are exposed via the wildfly management interface on http://localhost:9990/metrics
